codeQuality: High quality implementation with proper debounce logic, dimension comparison optimization, and comprehensive cleanup. The 150ms debounce delay is well-chosen within the 100-200ms requirement range. Verification logging added for Chrome DevTools Performance tab monitoring. Code follows existing patterns and includes proper memory leak prevention through debounce timer cleanup on component unmount.

VAL-PERF-001.evidence: Code review of TerminalCell.tsx lines 403-427 confirms: (1) ResizeObserver fires with 150ms debounce delay using fitDebounceRef.current timeout (line 425), (2) clearTimeout called on previous timeout before scheduling new one (lines 408-410), (3) Dimension comparison prevents redundant IPC calls when cols/rows unchanged (lines 419-424), (4) terminalResize IPC call and ptyProcess.resize() triggered only once per resize sequence with final dimensions. Console logging added for verification: '[TerminalCell {id}] Resizing terminal to {cols}x{rows} (debounced)' and '[TerminalCell {id}] Resize skipped - dimensions unchanged at {cols}x{rows}'. IPC handler in terminal.handlers.ts line 358 confirms ptyProcess.resize(cols, rows) called with correct dimensions.

VAL-FLOW-003.evidence: Implementation ensures no text loss during rapid resize through: (1) Dimension comparison guards against redundant resize calls that could corrupt text output (lines 419-424), (2) Debounce prevents excessive IPC spam during rapid resize gestures (commit 87b3935), (3) lastDimensionsRef.current tracks previous dimensions to ensure only actual size changes trigger terminal.resize() (line 421), (4) Proper cleanup of debounce timers on component unmount prevents memory leaks and orphaned callbacks (lines 558-562). Build verification passed with 'bun run build' showing 80 modules transformed without errors.

summary: Resize debouncing feature implementation passes all validation criteria. The worker successfully implemented a 150ms debounce delay on ResizeObserver callbacks in TerminalCell.tsx, reducing IPC calls from potentially 60+ per resize gesture to a single call. The implementation includes proper clearTimeout before scheduling new timeouts, dimension comparison to prevent redundant IPC calls, and comprehensive cleanup of debounce timers on component unmount. Both VAL-PERF-001 (resize debouncing) and VAL-FLOW-003 (no text loss during rapid resize) assertions are satisfied. Code quality is high with verification logging for Chrome DevTools Performance monitoring. Build completed successfully without TypeScript errors. No issues or shared state gaps identified.
